seoTitle: Delete Bank Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Safely exclude and delete unwanted bank feed transactions in QuickBooks Online, from duplicates to personal charges.
intro: Not every downloaded bank transaction belongs on your books, whether it's a duplicate, a personal charge, or a sync error. This guide shows the safe path in QuickBooks Online: excluding first, then permanently deleting from the archive.

steps.heading: Open Bank Transactions
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, and select your checking, savings, or credit card account.

steps.heading: Select the unwanted rows
steps.body: In the For Review queue, check the boxes next to duplicate or non-business transactions, selecting multiple for bulk processing if needed.

steps.heading: Exclude the transactions
steps.body: Click the Exclude action button in the header control ribbon to strip the rows from your main feed.

steps.heading: Open the Excluded tab
steps.body: Navigate to the Excluded workspace tab to review everything you've pulled out of the active feed.

steps.heading: Permanently delete
steps.body: Check the items you want gone for good and click Delete, then confirm the warning prompt to let your ledger recalculate.

faq.question: Why can't I delete a transaction directly from For Review?
faq.answer: QuickBooks requires you to first move it to the Excluded tab as a safety step before permanent deletion is available.

faq.question: What's the difference between excluding and deleting?
faq.answer: Excluding removes a transaction from your active feed but keeps it recoverable; deleting permanently purges it from your files.

faq.question: Can I undo an accidental exclusion?
faq.answer: Yes, the Undo feature lets you instantly recover a transaction you excluded by mistake.

faq.question: Will deleting a bank transaction affect my reconciliation?
faq.answer: It can, so review the warning prompt carefully and confirm the transaction isn't part of a completed reconciliation before deleting.
